import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 32000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem amc12a_2009_p25 (a : ℕ → ℝ) (h₀ : a 1 = 1) (h₁ : a 2 = 1 / Real.sqrt 3)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1))) : abs (a 2009) = 0 := by
  set s := Real.sqrt 3 with hs_def
  have hs_pos : (0 : ℝ) < s := Real.sqrt_pos_of_pos (by norm_num)
  have hs_ne : s ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt hs_pos
  have hss : s * s = 3 := Real.mul_self_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs_gt1 : 1 < s := by nlinarith [hss]
  have rec := h₂
  -- Compute a(3) = (s+1)/(s-1)
  have ha3 : a 3 = (s + 1) / (s - 1) := by
    have h := rec 1 le_rfl; simp only [show (1:ℕ)+2=3 from rfl, show (1:ℕ)+1=2 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₀, h₁]; field_simp; linarith
  -- Compute a(4)
  have ha4 : a 4 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 2 (by norm_num); simp only [show (2:ℕ)+2=4 from rfl, show (2:ℕ)+1=3 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₁, ha3]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * ((s + 1) / (s - 1)) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(5) = 0 (since a(3) + a(4) = 0)
  have ha5 : a 5 = 0 := by
    have h := rec 3 (by norm_num); simp only [show (3:ℕ)+2=5 from rfl, show (3:ℕ)+1=4 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha3, ha4]; simp
  -- a(6), a(7) simplified since a(5)=0
  have ha6 : a 6 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 4 (by norm_num); simp only [show (4:ℕ)+2=6 from rfl, show (4:ℕ)+1=5 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha4, ha5]; simp
  have ha7 : a 7 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 5 (by norm_num); simp only [show (5:ℕ)+2=7 from rfl, show (5:ℕ)+1=6 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha5, ha6]; simp
  -- a(8) = 1/s
  have ha8 : a 8 = 1 / s := by
    have h := rec 6 (by norm_num); simp only [show (6:ℕ)+2=8 from rfl, show (6:ℕ)+1=7 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha6, ha7]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [neg_mul_neg, sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(9) = -1
  have ha9 : a 9 = -1 := by
    have h := rec 7 (by norm_num); simp only [show (7:ℕ)+2=9 from rfl, show (7:ℕ)+1=8 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha7, ha8]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * (1 / s) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(10) = s - 2
  have ha10 : a 10 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 8 (by norm_num); simp only [show (8:ℕ)+2=10 from rfl, show (8:ℕ)+1=9 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha8, ha9]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(11) = -s
  have ha11 : a 11 = -s := by
    have h := rec 9 (by norm_num); simp only [show (9:ℕ)+2=11 from rfl, show (9:ℕ)+1=10 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha9, ha10]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(12) = -(2+s)
  have ha12 : a 12 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := rec 10 (by norm_num); simp only [show (10:ℕ)+2=12 from rfl, show (10:ℕ)+1=11 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha10, ha11]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (-s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(13) = 1
  have ha13 : a 13 = 1 := by
    have h := rec 11 (by norm_num); simp only [show (11:ℕ)+2=13 from rfl, show (11:ℕ)+1=12 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha11, ha12]
    have hd : 1 - (-s) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(14) = -1/s
  have ha14 : a 14 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := rec 12 (by norm_num); simp only [show (12:ℕ)+2=14 from rfl, show (12:ℕ)+1=13 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha12, ha13]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) = 2 - s
  have ha15 : a 15 = 2 - s := by
    have h := rec 13 (by norm_num); simp only [show (13:ℕ)+2=15 from rfl, show (13:ℕ)+1=14 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha13, ha14]
    have hd : 1 - 1 * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(16) = s - 2
  have ha16 : a 16 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 14 (by norm_num); simp only [show (14:ℕ)+2=16 from rfl, show (14:ℕ)+1=15 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha14, ha15]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (2 - s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) + a(16) = 0 → a(17) = 0 (KEY!)
  have ha17 : a 17 = 0 := by
    have h := rec 15 (by norm_num); simp only [show (15:ℕ)+2=17 from rfl, show (15:ℕ)+1=16 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha15, ha16]; simp
  -- Continue for periodicity: a(18)..a(26)
  have ha18 : a 18 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 16 (by norm_num); simp only [show (16:ℕ)+2=18 from rfl, show (16:ℕ)+1=17 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha16, ha17]; simp
  have ha19 : a 19 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 17 (by norm_num); simp only [show (17:ℕ)+2=19 from rfl, show (17:ℕ)+1=18 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha17, ha18]; simp
  have ha20 : a 20 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := rec 18 (by norm_num); simp only [show (18:ℕ)+2=20 from rfl, show (18:ℕ)+1=19 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha18, ha19]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha21 : a 21 = -1 := by
    have h := rec 19 (by norm_num); simp only [show (19:ℕ)+2=21 from rfl, show (19:ℕ)+1=20 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha19, ha20]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha22 : a 22 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := rec 20 (by norm_num); simp only [show (20:ℕ)+2=22 from rfl, show (20:ℕ)+1=21 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha20, ha21]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha23 : a 23 = s := by
    have h := rec 21 (by norm_num); simp only [show (21:ℕ)+2=23 from rfl, show (21:ℕ)+1=22 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha21, ha22]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha24 : a 24 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 22 (by norm_num); simp only [show (22:ℕ)+2=24 from rfl, show (22:ℕ)+1=23 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha22, ha23]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * s ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha25 : a 25 = 1 := by
    have h := rec 23 (by norm_num); simp only [show (23:ℕ)+2=25 from rfl, show (23:ℕ)+1=24 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha23, ha24]
    have hd : 1 - s * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha26 : a 26 = 1 / s := by
    have h := rec 24 (by norm_num); simp only [show (24:ℕ)+2=26 from rfl, show (24:ℕ)+1=25 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha24, ha25]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- Period 24 by strong induction
  have hperiod : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
    suffices ∀ k, (1 ≤ k → a (k + 24) = a k) ∧ (1 ≤ k + 1 → a (k + 25) = a (k + 1)) by
      intro n hn; exact (this n).1 hn
    intro k
    induction k with
    | zero =>
      exact ⟨by omega, by intro; simp [show 0+25=25 from rfl, show 0+1=1 from rfl]; rw [ha25, h₀]⟩
    | succ m ih =>
      constructor
      · intro hm1; exact ih.2 hm1
      · intro hm2
        -- Need a(m+2+24) = a(m+2)
        -- We know a(m+1+24) = a(m+1) from ih.2, and a(m+24) = a(m) from ih.1 (when m≥1)
        -- Or from base case when m=0
        by_cases hm0 : m = 0
        · subst hm0; simp [show 0+1+25=26 from rfl, show 0+1+1=2 from rfl]
          rw [ha26, h₁]
        · have hm1 : 1 ≤ m := by omega
          have ihm := ih.1 hm1
          have ihsm := ih.2 (by omega : 1 ≤ m + 1)
          have hrec_m := rec m hm1
          have hrec_m24 := rec (m + 24) (by omega)
          simp only [show m+2 = m+1+1 from by ring] at hrec_m
          simp only [show m+24+2 = (m+1+1)+24 from by ring, show m+24+1 = (m+1)+24 from by ring,
                      show (m+24 : ℕ) = m+24 from rfl] at hrec_m24
          rw [show m + 1 + 1 + 24 = m + 1 + 25 from by ring] at hrec_m24 ⊢
          rw [hrec_m24, ihm, ihsm, ← hrec_m]
  -- 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83
  have ha2009 : a 2009 = a 17 := by
    have : ∀ m, a (17 + 24 * m) = a 17 := by
      intro m; induction m with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k ih =>
        rw [show 17 + 24 * (k + 1) = (17 + 24 * k) + 24 from by ring]
        exact (hperiod _ (by omega)).trans ih
    have h2009 : 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83 := by norm_num
    rw [h2009]; exact this 83
  rw [ha2009, ha17, abs_zero]

  -- a(5) = 0 (since a(3) + a(4) = 0)
  have ha5 : a 5 = 0 := by
    have h := rec 3 (by norm_num); simp only [show (3:ℕ)+2=5 from rfl, show (3:ℕ)+1=4 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha3, ha4]; simp
  -- a(6), a(7) simplified since a(5)=0
  have ha6 : a 6 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 4 (by norm_num); simp only [show (4:ℕ)+2=6 from rfl, show (4:ℕ)+1=5 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha4, ha5]; simp
  have ha7 : a 7 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 5 (by norm_num); simp only [show (5:ℕ)+2=7 from rfl, show (5:ℕ)+1=6 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha5, ha6]; simp
  -- a(8) = 1/s
  have ha8 : a 8 = 1 / s := by
    have h := rec 6 (by norm_num); simp only [show (6:ℕ)+2=8 from rfl, show (6:ℕ)+1=7 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha6, ha7]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [neg_mul_neg, sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(9) = -1
  have ha9 : a 9 = -1 := by
    have h := rec 7 (by norm_num); simp only [show (7:ℕ)+2=9 from rfl, show (7:ℕ)+1=8 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha7, ha8]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * (1 / s) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(10) = s - 2
  have ha10 : a 10 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 8 (by norm_num); simp only [show (8:ℕ)+2=10 from rfl, show (8:ℕ)+1=9 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha8, ha9]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(11) = -s
  have ha11 : a 11 = -s := by
    have h := rec 9 (by norm_num); simp only [show (9:ℕ)+2=11 from rfl, show (9:ℕ)+1=10 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha9, ha10]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(12) = -(2+s)
  have ha12 : a 12 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := rec 10 (by norm_num); simp only [show (10:ℕ)+2=12 from rfl, show (10:ℕ)+1=11 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha10, ha11]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (-s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(13) = 1
  have ha13 : a 13 = 1 := by
    have h := rec 11 (by norm_num); simp only [show (11:ℕ)+2=13 from rfl, show (11:ℕ)+1=12 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha11, ha12]
    have hd : 1 - (-s) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(14) = -1/s
  have ha14 : a 14 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := rec 12 (by norm_num); simp only [show (12:ℕ)+2=14 from rfl, show (12:ℕ)+1=13 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha12, ha13]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) = 2 - s
  have ha15 : a 15 = 2 - s := by
    have h := rec 13 (by norm_num); simp only [show (13:ℕ)+2=15 from rfl, show (13:ℕ)+1=14 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha13, ha14]
    have hd : 1 - 1 * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(16) = s - 2
  have ha16 : a 16 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 14 (by norm_num); simp only [show (14:ℕ)+2=16 from rfl, show (14:ℕ)+1=15 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha14, ha15]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (2 - s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) + a(16) = 0 → a(17) = 0 (KEY!)
  have ha17 : a 17 = 0 := by
    have h := rec 15 (by norm_num); simp only [show (15:ℕ)+2=17 from rfl, show (15:ℕ)+1=16 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha15, ha16]; simp
  -- Continue for periodicity: a(18)..a(26)
  have ha18 : a 18 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 16 (by norm_num); simp only [show (16:ℕ)+2=18 from rfl, show (16:ℕ)+1=17 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha16, ha17]; simp
  have ha19 : a 19 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 17 (by norm_num); simp only [show (17:ℕ)+2=19 from rfl, show (17:ℕ)+1=18 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha17, ha18]; simp
  have ha20 : a 20 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := rec 18 (by norm_num); simp only [show (18:ℕ)+2=20 from rfl, show (18:ℕ)+1=19 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha18, ha19]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha21 : a 21 = -1 := by
    have h := rec 19 (by norm_num); simp only [show (19:ℕ)+2=21 from rfl, show (19:ℕ)+1=20 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha19, ha20]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha22 : a 22 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := rec 20 (by norm_num); simp only [show (20:ℕ)+2=22 from rfl, show (20:ℕ)+1=21 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha20, ha21]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha23 : a 23 = s := by
    have h := rec 21 (by norm_num); simp only [show (21:ℕ)+2=23 from rfl, show (21:ℕ)+1=22 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha21, ha22]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha24 : a 24 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 22 (by norm_num); simp only [show (22:ℕ)+2=24 from rfl, show (22:ℕ)+1=23 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha22, ha23]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * s ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha25 : a 25 = 1 := by
    have h := rec 23 (by norm_num); simp only [show (23:ℕ)+2=25 from rfl, show (23:ℕ)+1=24 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha23, ha24]
    have hd : 1 - s * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha26 : a 26 = 1 / s := by
    have h := rec 24 (by norm_num); simp only [show (24:ℕ)+2=26 from rfl, show (24:ℕ)+1=25 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha24, ha25]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- Period 24 by strong induction
  have hperiod : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
    suffices ∀ k, (1 ≤ k → a (k + 24) = a k) ∧ (1 ≤ k + 1 → a (k + 25) = a (k + 1)) by
      intro n hn; exact (this n).1 hn
    intro k
    induction k with
    | zero =>
      exact ⟨by omega, by intro; simp [show 0+25=25 from rfl, show 0+1=1 from rfl]; rw [ha25, h₀]⟩
    | succ m ih =>
      constructor
      · intro hm1; exact ih.2 hm1
      · intro hm2
        -- Need a(m+2+24) = a(m+2)
        -- We know a(m+1+24) = a(m+1) from ih.2, and a(m+24) = a(m) from ih.1 (when m≥1)
        -- Or from base case when m=0
        by_cases hm0 : m = 0
        · subst hm0; simp [show 0+1+25=26 from rfl, show 0+1+1=2 from rfl]
          rw [ha26, h₁]
        · have hm1 : 1 ≤ m := by omega
          have ihm := ih.1 hm1
          have ihsm := ih.2 (by omega : 1 ≤ m + 1)
          have hrec_m := rec m hm1
          have hrec_m24 := rec (m + 24) (by omega)
          simp only [show m+2 = m+1+1 from by ring] at hrec_m
          simp only [show m+24+2 = (m+1+1)+24 from by ring, show m+24+1 = (m+1)+24 from by ring,
                      show (m+24 : ℕ) = m+24 from rfl] at hrec_m24
          rw [show m + 1 + 1 + 24 = m + 1 + 25 from by ring] at hrec_m24 ⊢
          rw [hrec_m24, ihm, ihsm, ← hrec_m]
  -- 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83
  have ha2009 : a 2009 = a 17 := by
    have : ∀ m, a (17 + 24 * m) = a 17 := by
      intro m; induction m with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k ih =>
        rw [show 17 + 24 * (k + 1) = (17 + 24 * k) + 24 from by ring]
        exact (hperiod _ (by omega)).trans ih
    have h2009 : 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83 := by norm_num
    rw [h2009]; exact this 83
  rw [ha2009, ha17, abs_zero]

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 16000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem amc12a_2009_p25 (a : ℕ → ℝ) (h₀ : a 1 = 1) (h₁ : a 2 = 1 / Real.sqrt 3)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1))) : abs (a 2009) = 0 := by
  set s := Real.sqrt 3 with hs_def
  have hs_pos : 0 < s := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr (by norm_num : (3:ℝ) > 0)
  have hs_ne : s ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt hs_pos
  have hs2 : s * s = 3 := Real.mul_self_sqrt (by norm_num : (3:ℝ) ≥ 0)
  have hs_gt1 : 1 < s := by nlinarith [hs2]
  -- a(3)
  have ha3 : a 3 = 2 + s := by
    have h3 := h₂ 1 le_rfl; rw [show (1:ℕ)+2=3 from rfl, h₀, h₁] at h3; rw [h3, one_mul]
    have hd : (1:ℝ) - 1 / s ≠ 0 := by
      field_simp; linarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hs2]
  -- a(4)
  have ha4 : a 4 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h4 := h₂ 2 (by norm_num); rw [show (2:ℕ)+2=4 from rfl, h₁, ha3] at h4; rw [h4]
    have hd : (1:ℝ) - 1 / s * (2 + s) ≠ 0 := by
      field_simp; nlinarith [hs2]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hs2]
  -- a(5)
  have ha5 : a 5 = 0 := by
    have h5 := h₂ 3 (by norm_num); rw [show (3:ℕ)+2=5 from rfl, ha3, ha4] at h5; rw [h5]; ring_nf
  -- a(6)
  have ha6 : a 6 = -(2 + s) := by
    have := h₂ 4 (by norm_num); rw [show (4:ℕ)+2=6 from rfl, ha4, ha5] at this; rw [this]; ring_nf
  -- a(7)
  have ha7 : a 7 = -(2 + s) := by
    have := h₂ 5 (by norm_num); rw [show (5:ℕ)+2=7 from rfl, ha5, ha6] at this; rw [this]; ring_nf
  -- a(8)
  have ha8 : a 8 = 1 / s := by
    have := h₂ 6 (by norm_num); rw [show (6:ℕ)+2=8 from rfl, ha6, ha7] at this; rw [this]
    have hd : (1:ℝ) - -(2 + s) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hs2]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hs2]
  -- a(9)
  have ha9 : a 9 = -1 := by
    have := h₂ 7 (by norm_num); rw [show (7:ℕ)+2=9 from rfl, ha7, ha8] at this; rw [this]
    have hd : (1:ℝ) - -(2 + s) * (1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hs2]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hs2]
  -- a(10)
  have ha10 : a 10 = s - 2 := by

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 32000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem amc12a_2009_p25 (a : ℕ → ℝ) (h₀ : a 1 = 1) (h₁ : a 2 = 1 / Real.sqrt 3)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1))) : abs (a 2009) = 0 := by
  set s := Real.sqrt 3 with hs_def
  have hs_pos : (0 : ℝ) < s := Real.sqrt_pos_of_pos (by norm_num)
  have hs_ne : s ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt hs_pos
  have hss : s * s = 3 := Real.mul_self_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs_gt1 : 1 < s := by nlinarith [hss]
  have rec := h₂
  -- Compute a(3) = (s+1)/(s-1)
  have ha3 : a 3 = (s + 1) / (s - 1) := by
    have h := rec 1 le_rfl; simp only [show (1:ℕ)+2=3 from rfl, show (1:ℕ)+1=2 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₀, h₁]; field_simp; linarith
  -- Compute a(4)
  have ha4 : a 4 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 2 (by norm_num); simp only [show (2:ℕ)+2=4 from rfl, show (2:ℕ)+1=3 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₁, ha3]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * ((s + 1) / (s - 1)) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(5) = 0 (since a(3) + a(4) = 0)
  have ha5 : a 5 = 0 := by
    have h := rec 3 (by norm_num); simp only [show (3:ℕ)+2=5 from rfl, show (3:ℕ)+1=4 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha3, ha4]; simp
  -- a(6), a(7) simplified since a(5)=0
  have ha6 : a 6 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 4 (by norm_num); simp only [show (4:ℕ)+2=6 from rfl, show (4:ℕ)+1=5 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha4, ha5]; simp
  have ha7 : a 7 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := rec 5 (by norm_num); simp only [show (5:ℕ)+2=7 from rfl, show (5:ℕ)+1=6 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha5, ha6]; simp
  -- a(8) = 1/s
  have ha8 : a 8 = 1 / s := by
    have h := rec 6 (by norm_num); simp only [show (6:ℕ)+2=8 from rfl, show (6:ℕ)+1=7 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha6, ha7]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [neg_mul_neg, sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(9) = -1
  have ha9 : a 9 = -1 := by
    have h := rec 7 (by norm_num); simp only [show (7:ℕ)+2=9 from rfl, show (7:ℕ)+1=8 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha7, ha8]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * (1 / s) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(10) = s - 2
  have ha10 : a 10 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 8 (by norm_num); simp only [show (8:ℕ)+2=10 from rfl, show (8:ℕ)+1=9 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha8, ha9]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(11) = -s
  have ha11 : a 11 = -s := by
    have h := rec 9 (by norm_num); simp only [show (9:ℕ)+2=11 from rfl, show (9:ℕ)+1=10 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha9, ha10]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(12) = -(2+s)
  have ha12 : a 12 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := rec 10 (by norm_num); simp only [show (10:ℕ)+2=12 from rfl, show (10:ℕ)+1=11 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha10, ha11]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (-s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(13) = 1
  have ha13 : a 13 = 1 := by
    have h := rec 11 (by norm_num); simp only [show (11:ℕ)+2=13 from rfl, show (11:ℕ)+1=12 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha11, ha12]
    have hd : 1 - (-s) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(14) = -1/s
  have ha14 : a 14 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := rec 12 (by norm_num); simp only [show (12:ℕ)+2=14 from rfl, show (12:ℕ)+1=13 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha12, ha13]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) = 2 - s
  have ha15 : a 15 = 2 - s := by
    have h := rec 13 (by norm_num); simp only [show (13:ℕ)+2=15 from rfl, show (13:ℕ)+1=14 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha13, ha14]
    have hd : 1 - 1 * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(16) = s - 2
  have ha16 : a 16 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 14 (by norm_num); simp only [show (14:ℕ)+2=16 from rfl, show (14:ℕ)+1=15 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha14, ha15]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (2 - s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) + a(16) = 0 → a(17) = 0 (KEY!)
  have ha17 : a 17 = 0 := by
    have h := rec 15 (by norm_num); simp only [show (15:ℕ)+2=17 from rfl, show (15:ℕ)+1=16 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha15, ha16]; simp
  -- Continue for periodicity: a(18)..a(26)
  have ha18 : a 18 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 16 (by norm_num); simp only [show (16:ℕ)+2=18 from rfl, show (16:ℕ)+1=17 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha16, ha17]; simp
  have ha19 : a 19 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 17 (by norm_num); simp only [show (17:ℕ)+2=19 from rfl, show (17:ℕ)+1=18 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha17, ha18]; simp
  have ha20 : a 20 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := rec 18 (by norm_num); simp only [show (18:ℕ)+2=20 from rfl, show (18:ℕ)+1=19 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha18, ha19]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha21 : a 21 = -1 := by
    have h := rec 19 (by norm_num); simp only [show (19:ℕ)+2=21 from rfl, show (19:ℕ)+1=20 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha19, ha20]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha22 : a 22 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := rec 20 (by norm_num); simp only [show (20:ℕ)+2=22 from rfl, show (20:ℕ)+1=21 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha20, ha21]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha23 : a 23 = s := by
    have h := rec 21 (by norm_num); simp only [show (21:ℕ)+2=23 from rfl, show (21:ℕ)+1=22 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha21, ha22]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha24 : a 24 = s - 2 := by
    have h := rec 22 (by norm_num); simp only [show (22:ℕ)+2=24 from rfl, show (22:ℕ)+1=23 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha22, ha23]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * s ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha25 : a 25 = 1 := by
    have h := rec 23 (by norm_num); simp only [show (23:ℕ)+2=25 from rfl, show (23:ℕ)+1=24 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha23, ha24]
    have hd : 1 - s * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha26 : a 26 = 1 / s := by
    have h := rec 24 (by norm_num); simp only [show (24:ℕ)+2=26 from rfl, show (24:ℕ)+1=25 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha24, ha25]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- Period 24 by strong induction
  have hperiod : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
    suffices ∀ k, (1 ≤ k → a (k + 24) = a k) ∧ (1 ≤ k + 1 → a (k + 25) = a (k + 1)) by
      intro n hn; exact (this n).1 hn
    intro k
    induction k with
    | zero =>
      exact ⟨by omega, by intro; simp [show 0+25=25 from rfl, show 0+1=1 from rfl]; rw [ha25, h₀]⟩
    | succ m ih =>
      constructor
      · intro hm1; exact ih.2 hm1
      · intro hm2
        -- Need a(m+2+24) = a(m+2)
        -- We know a(m+1+24) = a(m+1) from ih.2, and a(m+24) = a(m) from ih.1 (when m≥1)
        -- Or from base case when m=0
        by_cases hm0 : m = 0
        · subst hm0; simp [show 0+1+25=26 from rfl, show 0+1+1=2 from rfl]
          rw [ha26, h₁]
        · have hm1 : 1 ≤ m := by omega
          have ihm := ih.1 hm1
          have ihsm := ih.2 (by omega : 1 ≤ m + 1)
          have hrec_m := rec m hm1
          have hrec_m24 := rec (m + 24) (by omega)
          simp only [show m+2 = m+1+1 from by ring] at hrec_m
          simp only [show m+24+2 = (m+1+1)+24 from by ring, show m+24+1 = (m+1)+24 from by ring,
                      show (m+24 : ℕ) = m+24 from rfl] at hrec_m24
          rw [show m + 1 + 1 + 24 = m + 1 + 25 from by ring] at hrec_m24 ⊢
          rw [hrec_m24, ihm, ihsm, ← hrec_m]
  -- 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83
  have ha2009 : a 2009 = a 17 := by
    have : ∀ m, a (17 + 24 * m) = a 17 := by
      intro m; induction m with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k ih =>
        rw [show 17 + 24 * (k + 1) = (17 + 24 * k) + 24 from by ring]
        exact (hperiod _ (by omega)).trans ih
    have h2009 : 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83 := by norm_num
    rw [h2009]; exact this 83
  rw [ha2009, ha17, abs_zero]

  have ha22 : a 22 = -(2 + s) := by

  have ha23 : a 23 = s := by
    have h := h₂ 21 (by norm_num)

    rw [h, ha21, ha22]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha24 : a 24 = s - 2 := by
    have h := h₂ 22 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (22:ℕ)+2=24 from rfl, show (22:ℕ)+1=23 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha22, ha23]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * s ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha25 : a 25 = 1 := by

    have hd : 1 - s * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]

  have ha26 : a 26 = 1 / s := by

    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- Period 24: (a 25, a 26) = (a 1, a 2) ⟹ a(n+24) = a(n) for n ≥ 1
  have hperiod : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
    -- Prove pairs: ∀ k ≥ 1, a(k+24) = a(k) ∧ a(k+1+24) = a(k+1)
    suffices ∀ k, 1 ≤ k → a (k + 24) = a k by exact this
    intro k hk
    -- Strong induction
    have claim : ∀ j, j ≤ k → 1 ≤ j → a (j + 24) = a j := by
      intro j hj hj1
      induction j with
      | zero => omega
      | succ m ihm =>
        match m with
        | 0 => -- j = 1
          rw [show 1 + 24 = 25 from rfl]; linarith [ha25, h₀]
        | 1 => -- j = 2
          rw [show 2 + 24 = 26 from rfl]; linarith [ha26, h₁]
        | m + 2 => -- j = m+3 ≥ 3
          have hm1 : 1 ≤ m + 1 := by omega
          have hm2 : 1 ≤ m + 2 := by omega

          have ih2 := ihm (by omega : m + 2 ≤ m + 2) (by omega : m + 2 ≤ k) hm2
          sorry
    exact claim k le_rfl hk
  sorry

  have ha25 : a 25 = 1 := by
    have h := h₂ 23 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (23:ℕ)+2=25 from rfl, show (23:ℕ)+1=24 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha23, ha24]
    have hd : 1 - s * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha26 : a 26 = 1 / s := by
    have h := h₂ 24 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (24:ℕ)+2=26 from rfl, show (24:ℕ)+1=25 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha24, ha25]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- Period 24: (a 25, a 26) = (a 1, a 2) ⟹ a(n+24) = a(n) for n ≥ 1
  have hperiod : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
    -- Prove pairs: ∀ k ≥ 1, a(k+24) = a(k) ∧ a(k+1+24) = a(k+1)
    suffices ∀ k, 1 ≤ k → a (k + 24) = a k by exact this
    intro k hk
    -- Strong induction
    have claim : ∀ j, j ≤ k → 1 ≤ j → a (j + 24) = a j := by
      intro j hj hj1
      induction j with
      | zero => omega
      | succ m ihm =>
        match m with
        | 0 => -- j = 1
          rw [show 1 + 24 = 25 from rfl]; linarith [ha25, h₀]
        | 1 => -- j = 2
          rw [show 2 + 24 = 26 from rfl]; linarith [ha26, h₁]
        | m + 2 => -- j = m+3 ≥ 3
          have hm1 : 1 ≤ m + 1 := by omega
          have hm2 : 1 ≤ m + 2 := by omega
          have ih1 := ihm (by omega : m + 1 ≤ m + 1) (by omega : m + 1 ≤ k) hm1
          have ih2 := ihm (by omega : m + 2 ≤ m + 2) (by omega : m + 2 ≤ k) hm2
          sorry
    exact claim k le_rfl hk
  sorry

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 32000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem amc12a_2009_p25 (a : ℕ → ℝ) (h₀ : a 1 = 1) (h₁ : a 2 = 1 / Real.sqrt 3)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1))) : abs (a 2009) = 0 := by
  set s := Real.sqrt 3 with hs_def
  have hs_pos : (0 : ℝ) < s := Real.sqrt_pos_of_pos (by norm_num)
  have hs_ne : s ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt hs_pos
  have hss : s * s = 3 := Real.mul_self_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs_gt1 : 1 < s := by nlinarith [hss]
  -- Compute a(3) = (s+1)/(s-1)
  have ha3 : a 3 = (s + 1) / (s - 1) := by
    have h := h₂ 1 le_rfl; simp only [show (1:ℕ)+2=3 from rfl, show (1:ℕ)+1=2 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₀, h₁]; field_simp; linarith
  -- Compute a(4)
  have ha4 : a 4 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := h₂ 2 (by norm_num); simp only [show (2:ℕ)+2=4 from rfl, show (2:ℕ)+1=3 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₁, ha3]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * ((s + 1) / (s - 1)) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(5) = 0 (since a(3) + a(4) = 0)
  have ha5 : a 5 = 0 := by
    have h := h₂ 3 (by norm_num); simp only [show (3:ℕ)+2=5 from rfl, show (3:ℕ)+1=4 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha3, ha4]; simp
  -- a(6), a(7) simplified since a(5)=0
  have ha6 : a 6 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := h₂ 4 (by norm_num); simp only [show (4:ℕ)+2=6 from rfl, show (4:ℕ)+1=5 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha4, ha5]; simp
  have ha7 : a 7 = -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) := by
    have h := h₂ 5 (by norm_num); simp only [show (5:ℕ)+2=7 from rfl, show (5:ℕ)+1=6 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha5, ha6]; simp
  -- a(8) = 1/s
  have ha8 : a 8 = 1 / s := by
    have h := h₂ 6 (by norm_num); simp only [show (6:ℕ)+2=8 from rfl, show (6:ℕ)+1=7 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha6, ha7]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [neg_mul_neg, sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(9) = -1
  have ha9 : a 9 = -1 := by
    have h := h₂ 7 (by norm_num); simp only [show (7:ℕ)+2=9 from rfl, show (7:ℕ)+1=8 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha7, ha8]
    have hd : 1 - -((s + 1) / (s - 1)) * (1 / s) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; field_simp at heq; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(10) = s - 2
  have ha10 : a 10 = s - 2 := by
    have h := h₂ 8 (by norm_num); simp only [show (8:ℕ)+2=10 from rfl, show (8:ℕ)+1=9 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha8, ha9]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(11) = -s
  have ha11 : a 11 = -s := by
    have h := h₂ 9 (by norm_num); simp only [show (9:ℕ)+2=11 from rfl, show (9:ℕ)+1=10 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha9, ha10]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(12) = -(2+s)
  have ha12 : a 12 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := h₂ 10 (by norm_num); simp only [show (10:ℕ)+2=12 from rfl, show (10:ℕ)+1=11 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha10, ha11]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (-s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(13) = 1
  have ha13 : a 13 = 1 := by
    have h := h₂ 11 (by norm_num); simp only [show (11:ℕ)+2=13 from rfl, show (11:ℕ)+1=12 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha11, ha12]
    have hd : 1 - (-s) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(14) = -1/s
  have ha14 : a 14 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := h₂ 12 (by norm_num); simp only [show (12:ℕ)+2=14 from rfl, show (12:ℕ)+1=13 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha12, ha13]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) = 2 - s
  have ha15 : a 15 = 2 - s := by
    have h := h₂ 13 (by norm_num); simp only [show (13:ℕ)+2=15 from rfl, show (13:ℕ)+1=14 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha13, ha14]
    have hd : 1 - 1 * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(16) = s - 2
  have ha16 : a 16 = s - 2 := by
    have h := h₂ 14 (by norm_num); simp only [show (14:ℕ)+2=16 from rfl, show (14:ℕ)+1=15 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha14, ha15]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (2 - s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(15) + a(16) = 0 → a(17) = 0 (KEY!)
  have ha17 : a 17 = 0 := by
    have h := h₂ 15 (by norm_num); simp only [show (15:ℕ)+2=17 from rfl, show (15:ℕ)+1=16 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha15, ha16]; simp
  -- Continue for periodicity: a(18)..a(26)
  have ha18 : a 18 = s - 2 := by
    have h := h₂ 16 (by norm_num); simp only [show (16:ℕ)+2=18 from rfl, show (16:ℕ)+1=17 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha16, ha17]; simp
  have ha19 : a 19 = s - 2 := by
    have h := h₂ 17 (by norm_num); simp only [show (17:ℕ)+2=19 from rfl, show (17:ℕ)+1=18 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha17, ha18]; simp
  have ha20 : a 20 = -(1 / s) := by
    have h := h₂ 18 (by norm_num); simp only [show (18:ℕ)+2=20 from rfl, show (18:ℕ)+1=19 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha18, ha19]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha21 : a 21 = -1 := by
    have h := h₂ 19 (by norm_num); simp only [show (19:ℕ)+2=21 from rfl, show (19:ℕ)+1=20 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha19, ha20]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * -(1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha22 : a 22 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := h₂ 20 (by norm_num); simp only [show (20:ℕ)+2=22 from rfl, show (20:ℕ)+1=21 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha20, ha21]
    have hd : 1 - -(1 / s) * (-1) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha23 : a 23 = s := by
    have h := h₂ 21 (by norm_num); simp only [show (21:ℕ)+2=23 from rfl, show (21:ℕ)+1=22 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha21, ha22]
    have hd : 1 - (-1) * -(2 + s) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha24 : a 24 = s - 2 := by
    have h := h₂ 22 (by norm_num); simp only [show (22:ℕ)+2=24 from rfl, show (22:ℕ)+1=23 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha22, ha23]
    have hd : 1 - -(2 + s) * s ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha25 : a 25 = 1 := by
    have h := h₂ 23 (by norm_num); simp only [show (23:ℕ)+2=25 from rfl, show (23:ℕ)+1=24 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha23, ha24]
    have hd : 1 - s * (s - 2) ≠ 0 := by nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; nlinarith [hss]
  have ha26 : a 26 = 1 / s := by
    have h := h₂ 24 (by norm_num); simp only [show (24:ℕ)+2=26 from rfl, show (24:ℕ)+1=25 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha24, ha25]
    have hd : 1 - (s - 2) * 1 ≠ 0 := by nlinarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- Period 24 by strong induction
  have hperiod : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
    suffices ∀ k, (1 ≤ k → a (k + 24) = a k) ∧ (1 ≤ k + 1 → a (k + 25) = a (k + 1)) by
      intro n hn; exact (this n).1 hn
    intro k
    induction k with
    | zero =>
      exact ⟨by omega, by intro; simp [show 0+25=25 from rfl, show 0+1=1 from rfl]; rw [ha25, h₀]⟩
    | succ m ih =>
      constructor
      · intro hm1; exact ih.2 hm1
      · intro hm2
        -- Need a(m+2+24) = a(m+2)
        -- We know a(m+1+24) = a(m+1) from ih.2, and a(m+24) = a(m) from ih.1 (when m≥1)
        -- Or from base case when m=0
        by_cases hm0 : m = 0
        · subst hm0; simp [show 0+1+25=26 from rfl, show 0+1+1=2 from rfl]
          rw [ha26, h₁]
        · have hm1 : 1 ≤ m := by omega
          have ihm := ih.1 hm1
          have ihsm := ih.2 (by omega : 1 ≤ m + 1)
          have hrec_m := h₂ m hm1
          have hrec_m24 := h₂ (m + 24) (by omega)
          simp only [show m+2 = m+1+1 from by ring] at hrec_m
          simp only [show m+24+2 = (m+1+1)+24 from by ring, show m+24+1 = (m+1)+24 from by ring,
                      show (m+24 : ℕ) = m+24 from rfl] at hrec_m24
          rw [show m + 1 + 1 + 24 = m + 1 + 25 from by ring] at hrec_m24 ⊢
          rw [hrec_m24, ihm, ihsm, ← hrec_m]
  -- 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83
  have ha2009 : a 2009 = a 17 := by
    have : ∀ m, a (17 + 24 * m) = a 17 := by
      intro m; induction m with
      | zero => simp
      | succ k ih =>
        rw [show 17 + 24 * (k + 1) = (17 + 24 * k) + 24 from by ring]
        exact (hperiod _ (by omega)).trans ih
    have h2009 : 2009 = 17 + 24 * 83 := by norm_num
    rw [h2009]; exact this 83
  rw [ha2009, ha17, abs_zero]

import Mathlib

-- Test the periodicity argument in isolation
example (a : ℕ → ℝ)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1)))
  (base1 : a 25 = a 1)
  (base2 : a 26 = a 2) :
  ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
  have step : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n → a (n + 25) = a (n + 1) → a (n + 26) = a (n + 2) := by
    intro n hn ih1 ih2
    have hrec := h₂ n hn
    have hrec24 := h₂ (n + 24) (by omega)
    simp only [show n + 24 + 2 = n + 26 from by omega, show n + 24 + 1 = n + 25 from by omega] at hrec24
    rw [hrec24, ih1, ih2]; exact hrec.symm
  suffices ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n ∧ a (n + 1 + 24) = a (n + 1) by
    intro n hn; exact (this n hn).1
  intro n hn
  induction n with
  | zero => omega
  | succ m ih =>
    constructor
    · -- a (m+1+24) = a (m+1)
      rcases Nat.eq_or_gt_of_le hn with rfl | hm
      · -- m + 1 = 1, i.e. m = 0
        exact base1
      · exact (ih (by omega)).2
    · -- a (m+2+24) = a (m+2)
      rcases Nat.eq_or_gt_of_le hn with rfl | hm
      · -- m + 1 = 1
        exact base2
      · rcases Nat.eq_or_gt_of_le (Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le Nat.lt.base hm) with rfl | hm2
        · -- m + 1 = 2
          exact step 1 le_rfl base1 base2
        · -- m ≥ 2
          have prev := ih (by omega)
          exact step (m + 1) (by omega) prev.2 ((ih (by omega)).2 |>.symm ▸ sorry)

input.content:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000

example (a : ℕ → ℝ)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1)))
  (base1 : a 25 = a 1)
  (base2 : a 26 = a 2) :
  ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 24) = a n := by
  -- Prove pairs: ∀ k, a(k+25)=a(k+1) ∧ a(k+26)=a(k+2)
  have pairs : ∀ k, a (k + 25) = a (k + 1) ∧ a (k + 26) = a (k + 2) := by
    intro k; induction k with
    | zero => exact ⟨by simpa using base1, by simpa using base2⟩
    | succ m ih =>
      refine ⟨ih.2, ?_⟩
      have hrec := h₂ (m + 1) (by omega)
      rw [show m + 1 + 2 = m + 3 from by omega] at hrec
      have hrec24 := h₂ (m + 25) (by omega)
      rw [show m + 25 + 2 = m + 27 from by omega, show m + 25 + 1 = m + 26 from by omega,
          show (m + 25 : ℕ) = m + 1 + 24 from by omega] at hrec24
      rw [show m + 1 + 24 = m + 25 from by omega] at hrec24
      rw [show m + 1 + 1 + 24 = m + 26 from by omega] at *
      rw [hrec24, ih.1, ih.2]; linarith [hrec]
  intro n hn; rcases n with _ | m; omega
  rw [show m + 1 + 24 = m + 25 from by omega]; exact (pairs m).1

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 32000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem amc12a_2009_p25 (a : ℕ → ℝ) (h₀ : a 1 = 1) (h₁ : a 2 = 1 / Real.sqrt 3)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1))) :
    abs (a 2009) = 0 := by
  set s := Real.sqrt 3 with hs_def
  have hs_pos : (0 : ℝ) < s := Real.sqrt_pos_of_pos (by norm_num)
  have hs_ne : s ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt hs_pos
  have hss : s * s = 3 := Real.mul_self_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs_gt1 : 1 < s := by nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(3) = 2 + s
  have ha3 : a 3 = 2 + s := by
    have h := h₂ 1 le_rfl
    simp only [show (1:ℕ)+2=3 from rfl, show (1:ℕ)+1=2 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₀, h₁]
    have hd : 1 - 1 * (1 / s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(4) = -(2 + s)
  have ha4 : a 4 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := h₂ 2 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (2:ℕ)+2=4 from rfl, show (2:ℕ)+1=3 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₁, ha3]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * (2 + s) ≠ 0 := by field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(5) = 0
  have ha5 : a 5 = 0 := by
    have h := h₂ 3 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (3:ℕ)+2=5 from rfl, show (3:ℕ)+1=4 from rfl] at h

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 32000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem amc12a_2009_p25 (a : ℕ → ℝ) (h₀ : a 1 = 1) (h₁ : a 2 = 1 / Real.sqrt 3)
  (h₂ : ∀ n, 1 ≤ n → a (n + 2) = (a n + a (n + 1)) / (1 - a n * a (n + 1))) :
    abs (a 2009) = 0 := by
  set s := Real.sqrt 3 with hs_def
  have hs_pos : (0 : ℝ) < s := Real.sqrt_pos_of_pos (by norm_num)
  have hs_ne : s ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt hs_pos
  have hss : s * s = 3 := Real.mul_self_sqrt (by norm_num)
  have hs_gt1 : 1 < s := by nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(3) = 2 + s
  have ha3 : a 3 = 2 + s := by
    have h := h₂ 1 le_rfl
    simp only [show (1:ℕ)+2=3 from rfl, show (1:ℕ)+1=2 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₀, h₁]
    have hd : 1 - 1 * (1 / s) ≠ 0 := by intro h; field_simp at h; linarith
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(4) = -(2 + s)
  have ha4 : a 4 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := h₂ 2 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (2:ℕ)+2=4 from rfl, show (2:ℕ)+1=3 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, h₁, ha3]
    have hd : 1 - 1 / s * (2 + s) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [sub_ne_zero]; intro heq; have : s * (1 - 1 / s * (2 + s)) = 0 := by rw [heq]; ring
      field_simp at this; nlinarith [hss]
    rw [div_eq_iff hd]; field_simp; nlinarith [hss]
  -- a(5) = 0
  have ha5 : a 5 = 0 := by
    have h := h₂ 3 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (3:ℕ)+2=5 from rfl, show (3:ℕ)+1=4 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha3, ha4]; simp
  -- a(6), a(7) via a(5)=0
  have ha6 : a 6 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := h₂ 4 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (4:ℕ)+2=6 from rfl, show (4:ℕ)+1=5 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha4, ha5]; simp
  have ha7 : a 7 = -(2 + s) := by
    have h := h₂ 5 (by norm_num)
    simp only [show (5:ℕ)+2=7 from rfl, show (5:ℕ)+1=6 from rfl] at h
    rw [h, ha5, ha6]; simp
  -- a(8) = 1/s
  have ha8 : a 8 = 1 / s := by
    have h := h₂ 6 (by norm_num)

    have base2 : a 26 = a 2 := by linarith [ha26, h₁]
    have pairs : ∀ k, a (k + 25) = a (k + 1) ∧ a (k + 26) = a (k + 2) := by

## 2026-03-28 — agent0 (session 2)

22. **Period computation for tangent addition recurrence**: The recurrence a(n+2) = (a(n)+a(n+1))/(1-a(n)*a(n+1)) is the tangent addition formula. For a(1)=1, a(2)=1/√3, the sequence has period 24 (not 12). Key: a(5)=0 and a(17)=0, but the full period requires computing all 24 values because a(14) = -a(2) (anti-period 12 for some terms). Proving period 24 requires showing (a(25),a(26)) = (a(1),a(2)) then pair induction.

23. **div_eq_iff for clearing fractions in Lean**: When proving `f/g = c` where g ≠ 0, use `rw [div_eq_iff hg]` to get `f = c * g`, then `field_simp` or `nlinarith`. Much more reliable than trying `field_simp` directly on the original division.

24. **Nonzero denominator proofs**: `intro h; field_simp at h; nlinarith [hss]` is more reliable than `field_simp; nlinarith [hss]` for proving `expr ≠ 0`. The former converts `expr = 0` into a polynomial equation at the hypothesis, then derives contradiction.

25. **Pair induction for periodicity**: To prove a(n+T) = a(n) for all n, prove pairs `a(k+T+1) = a(k+1) ∧ a(k+T+2) = a(k+2)` by induction on k. Base: verify for k=0. Step: use previous pair + recurrence.
EOF
echo "LEARNINGS updated"

content.thinking:
This is an existential statement: prove there exists f : ℕ → ℕ with:
1. f(1) = 2
2. ∀ n, f(f(n)) = f(n) + n
3. ∀ n, f(n) < f(n+1) (strictly increasing)

The solution is the Wythoff/Zeckendorf function related to the golden ratio. Specifically, f(n) = n + ⌊nφ⌋ - ⌊(n-1)φ⌋ or something like that. But the standard solution is:


The key insight is to use the Zeckendorf representation—every positive integer can be uniquely written as a sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers. If I express n as F_{a₁} + F_{a₂} + ... + F_{aₖ} where the indices are non-consecutive, then f(n) shifts each Fibonacci index up by one to get the result.

Formalizing this in Lean would be extremely involved—I'd need to prove Zeckendorf's theorem itself, define the function through this representation, and verify all three properties. That's hundreds of lines of proof work. Let me step back and reconsider the problem more carefully to see if there's a simpler approach.

Looking at the statement again, I need to parse the quantifiers correctly. The way `∧` and `∀` interact here matters—in Lean 4, conjunction binds tighter than universal quantification, so `∀ n, f (f n) = f n + n ∧ ∀ n, f n < f (n + 1)` actually means for all n, both the functional equation holds AND there exists a separate universal quantifier over all n for the monotonicity condition. This is a subtle parsing issue that changes what I'm actually trying to prove. , f(f(n)) = f(n)+n) ∧ (∀ n, f(n) < f(n+1))

So I need to find a function f that satisfies both conditions. The Zeckendorf representation seems overly complicated for this. Let me try a simpler approach using the golden ratio φ ≈ 1.618. If I define f(n) = ⌊φ·n⌋ + 1, I can check whether this works: f(1) = 2, f(2) = 4, and f(f(1)) = f(2) = 4.

But that fails the first condition since f(1) + 1 = 3, not 4. Let me try f(n) = ⌊φ²·n⌋ instead, where φ² ≈ 2.618. This gives f(1) = 2, f(2) = 5, f(3) = 7, but again f(f(1)) = 5 while f(1) + 1 = 3, so this doesn't work either. The Zeckendorf construction must have some special property that makes the functional equation hold.

Actually, I think the right approach is using Beatty sequences. If I define A as the sequence of floor(nφ) and B as floor(nφ²), these two sequences partition the positive integers by Rayleigh's theorem. The key insight is that mapping elements from A to B via f(⌊nφ⌋) = ⌊nφ²⌋ gives exactly f(⌊nφ⌋) = ⌊nφ⌋ + n, which should satisfy the functional equation.

But when I try to extend this to f(⌊nφ²⌋), it breaks down—the formula doesn't work cleanly. Let me reconsider using Zeckendorf's theorem instead, where I define f by shifting the Zeckendorf representation of numbers, though that requires formalizing the theorem itself, which gets complicated.

Maybe I should pivot to a different problem entirely. The IMO 2006 P3 inequality looks interesting—it's asking to prove a sharp bound on a symmetric expression in three variables using either AM-GM or Schur's inequality, though the SOS decomposition approach seems intractable.

Looking back at what I have left, I'm weighing the tractability of each: the divisibility problem needs order theory and lifting-the-exponent lemma, the harmonic sum requires careful Finset manipulation, Vieta jumping needs well-founded induction, Zeckendorf is extremely involved, the inequality has no clean SOS path, and the remaining problems involve either trigonometric computation or transcendental equations. I need to pick whichever one I can actually formalize cleanly.

Let me focus on imo_1967_p3 since it's about product divisibility and might be more straightforward than the others. Setting up the problem statement with the natural number parameters, the sequence definition, and the primality constraint...

Now I'm parsing the divisibility claim itself and working through the operator precedence—the divisibility symbol binds looser than subtraction, so I need to figure out whether this is asking if the product of c values divides the difference between another product and a single term, or if there's a different grouping. i) - c k` would be computed per-element if it's inside the product. But the product of differences doesn't make sense mathematically. Let me re-read.

Looking at the original: `(∏ i ∈ Finset.Icc 1 n, c i) ∣ ∏ i ∈ Finset.Icc 1 n, c (m + i) - c k`

I think this parses as: `(∏ i ∈ Finset.Icc 1 n, c i) ∣ (∏ i ∈ Finset.Icc 1 n, (c (m + i) - c k))`

So we need to show ∏_{i=1}^n i(i+1) divides ∏_{i=1}^n ((m+i)(m+i+1) - k(k+1)).

Now I'm working out the product formula. The left side is ∏_{i=1}^n i(i+1), which factors as n! · (n+1)!. For the right side, I need to expand c(m+i) - c(k) = (m+i)(m+i+1) - k(k+1) and see if I can show the divisibility holds.

I'm factoring the difference of these triangular-like numbers and rewriting the products as factorials to make the divisibility argument clearer. I'm recognizing that these products of consecutive integers can be expressed as factorials times binomial coefficients, so the right side becomes n!² times the product of two binomial coefficients. Now I need to figure out what divisibility condition this imposes—specifically whether (n+1) divides the product of those binomial coefficients, and how the primality of k+m factors in. Let me check what Lifting the Exponent lemmas are available in Mathlib and see if any of them apply to this problem, then circle back to imo_1990_p3.

content.text: While the Mathlib search runs, let me try a different approach to imo_1990_p3 — a more direct computational proof for small cases:

input.content:
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 8000000

-- imo_1990_p3: n ≥ 2, n² ∣ 2^n + 1 → n = 3
-- Key facts:
-- 1. n must be odd (if n even, 4 | n² but 4 ∤ 2^n+1 since 2^n ≡ 0 mod 4)
-- 2. 3 | n (since n odd → 2^n ≡ -1 mod 3 → 3 | 2^n+1, and n² | 2^n+1 → 3 | n)
-- 3. Smallest prime p of n satisfies ord_p(2) = 2 → p = 3
-- 4. n = 3^a, LTE gives v₃(2^(3^a)+1) = 1+a, need 2a ≤ 1+a → a ≤ 1 → n = 3

-- Let's try a direct approach: bound n and check computationally
-- If n ≥ 4 and n² | 2^n+1, we derive contradiction

-- First, a helper: for n even, n² ∤ 2^n+1
theorem imo_1990_p3 (n : ℕ) (h₀ : 2 ≤ n) (h₁ : n ^ 2 ∣ 2 ^ n + 1) : n = 3 := by
  -- Step 1: n must be odd
  have hodd : ¬ 2 ∣ n := by
    intro heven
    have : 4 ∣ n ^ 2 := by
      obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := heven
      rw [hk]; ring_nf; exact ⟨k * k, by ring⟩
    have h4 : 4 ∣ 2 ^ n + 1 := dvd_trans this h₁
    have : 2 ^ n + 1 ≡ 1 [MOD 4] := by
      have : 4 ∣ 2 ^ n := by
        have : 2 ≤ n := h₀
        have : 2 ^ 2 ∣ 2 ^ n := Nat.pow_dvd_pow 2 this
        simpa using this
      omega
    omega
  sorry

    {n : ℕ} (hn : Odd n) :
    emultiplicity (↑p) (x ^ n + y ^ n) = emultiplicity (↑p) (x + y) + emultiplicity p n := by

  exact Int.emultiplicity_pow_sub_pow hp hp1 hxy hx n

theorem Nat.emultiplicity_pow_sub_pow {x y : ℕ} (hxy : p ∣ x - y) (hx : ¬p ∣ x) (n : ℕ) :
    emultiplicity p (x ^ n - y ^ n) = emultiplicity p (x - y) + emultiplicity p n := by

content.text:
LTE exists as `Nat.emultiplicity_pow_add_pow`. The type signature:
```
emultiplicity p (x^n + y^n) = emultiplicity p (x+y) + emultiplicity p n
```
when `p | x+y`, `¬p | x`, and `n` is odd.

For imo_1990_p3, I'd use this with p=3, x=2, y=1, n=n:
- emultiplicity 3 (2^n + 1^n) = emultiplicity 3 (2+1) + emultiplicity 3 n
- emultiplicity 3 (2^n + 1) = emultiplicity 3 3 + emultiplicity 3 n = 1 + emultiplicity 3 n

This is a key step. But I still need the multiplicative order argument to show n is a power of 3.

This is still very complex. Let me check the scoring result and then decide whether to continue with imo_1990_p3 or try another approach.

import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem imo_2006_p3 (a b c : ℝ) :
  a * b * (a ^ 2 - b ^ 2) + b * c * (b ^ 2 - c ^ 2) + c * a * (c ^ 2 - a ^ 2) ≤
  9 * Real.sqrt 2 / 32 * (a ^ 2 + b ^ 2 + c ^ 2) ^ 2 := by
  first
  | solve | norm_num
  | solve | field_simp; ring
  | solve | field_simp; linarith
  | solve | field_simp; norm_num
  | solve | linarith
  | solve | nlinarith
  | solve | simp_all
  | solve | simp_all; ring
  | solve | simp_all; omega
  | solve | simp_all; linarith
  | solve | simp_all; nlinarith
  | solve | simp_all; norm_num
  | solve | omega
  | solve | ring
  | solve | decide
  | solve | simp; ring
  | solve | simp; omega
  | solve | simp; norm_num
  | solve | push_cast; ring

content.content:
Copied mathd_algebra_437 from exp142_a3
import Mathlib

set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat

theorem mathd_algebra_437 (x y : ℝ) (n : ℤ) (h₀ : x ^ 3 = -45) (h₁ : y ^ 3 = -101) (h₂ : x < n)
  (h₃ : ↑n < y) : n = -4 := by
  -- The hypotheses are contradictory: x ≈ -3.56 > -4 > -4.66 ≈ y, but x < n < y
  exfalso
  -- x > -4: since x³ = -45 > -64 = (-4)³ and cube is monotone
  have hx : (-4 : ℝ) < x := by
    have key : (x + 4) * (x ^ 2 - 4 * x + 16) = x ^ 3 + 64 := by ring
    nlinarith [key, h₀, sq_nonneg (x - 2)]
  -- y < -4: since y³ = -101 < -64 = (-4)³ and cube is monotone
  have hy : y < (-4 : ℝ) := by
    have key : (y + 4) * (y ^ 2 - 4 * y + 16) = y ^ 3 + 64 := by ring
    nlinarith [key, h₁, sq_nonneg (y - 2)]
  -- Now: -4 < x < ↑n < y < -4, contradiction
  linarith [h₂, h₃]
